item.title: Trump’s coalition of the unwilling
item.description: US allies have a right to avoid being sucked into a war they never sought

item.title: Orcel’s brash M&A tactics inch UniCredit closer to success
item.description: Few shareholders will accept the miserly offer for Commerzbank — but that doesn’t matter

item.title: Labour must not lose my generation of voters
item.description: The defeat by the Greens in Greater Manchester demonstrates the danger of failing to address economic burdens

item.title: AI world models need to understand cause and effect
item.description: They should be able to map how reality works, not just how it looks

item.title: Why Trump is obsessed with this Chicago-based shoe label
item.description: The president may buy Florsheim’s cap-toe Oxfords for his cabinet members. But its parent company is suing his government over tariffs

item.title: What do we really gain from no-cry onions?
item.description: Frankensteining low-pungency vegetables is popular, but with food the longer way round is often more enjoyable

item.title: Why Hormuz will haunt us long after this war ends
item.description: Iran has shown that control of the strait gives it a stranglehold over the world economy

item.title: 2022 redux?
item.description: War makes it possible.

item.title: The UK is struggling to achieve its aims on clean energy
item.description: Our economy remains highly fossil fuel-dependent partly because we are not electrifying in the desired way

item.title: Was Revolut’s banking licence worth the wait?
item.description: Group would be wise to move gradually with its lending ambitions

item.title: Private credit is key to keep Main Street moving
item.description: Despite recent concerns, it helps sustain job creation and economic growth

item.title: Boards must feel they can think for themselves
item.description: More companies should use the flexibility in the UK corporate governance code

item.title: Highly skilled workers have been training AI — that comes at a cost
item.description: Students entering this new labour market should plan what they share, rethink competition and consider collective bargaining

item.title: Concierge Nation: welcome to white-glove America
item.description: Pay enough, and you can jump to the front of the queue for almost anything

item.title: Trump has undermined the US national security apparatus
item.description: War against Iran does not seem to have undergone the normal stress-testing

item.title: Iran war should prompt a North Sea rethink
item.description: Sweating the UK’s natural gas assets can support the country’s green transition

item.title: Trump’s ‘shock and war’ makes this economic crisis different
item.description: Conflict with Iran will leave deeper and more lasting scars than last year’s tariffs crisis

item.title: The endlessly fascinating office protected species
item.description: Being deemed close to the boss lets you get away with murder — until it doesn’t

item.title: All respect, Timothée, art is about more than popularity
item.description: Some of the outrage appears disingenuous but the actor is wrong in his dismissal of opera and ballet

item.title: Wealth taxes’ design flaws consign them to failure
item.description: It may be easier for countries sitting on large deficits to tighten the screws on existing levies

item.title: Higher oil prices are just a short-term worry
item.description: Long-term inflation expectations are staying steady

item.title: Sovereign AI is a bet on the economies of anti-scale
item.description: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers

item.title: Why Iran could become the next Iraq
item.description: The first Gulf war left a weakened regime in place without a political settlement — paving the way for future conflict

item.title: Are markets being too complacent about the Iran war?
item.description: Sometimes crises do not blow over as investors might hope

item.title: Donald Trump, globalist
item.description: It should not have taken the Iran war to finish the idea that he is ‘isolationist’
